title: Physics
intro: HKDSE-aligned physics pathways across compulsory and elective modules.

chapters.title: Thermal energy, temperature, and internal energy
chapters.description: Distinguish heat, temperature, and internal energy using particle and energy models.

chapters.title: Heat transfer: conduction, convection, and radiation
chapters.description: Compare thermal transfer processes and evaluate insulation strategies in real systems.

chapters.title: Change of state and latent heat
chapters.description: Model phase changes with heating curves and latent heat calculations.

chapters.title: Gas laws and kinetic theory
chapters.description: Connect Boyle's and Charles's laws to particle motion and absolute temperature.

chapters.title: Position, velocity, acceleration, and motion graphs
chapters.description: Interpret and connect displacement-time, velocity-time, and acceleration-time graphs.

chapters.title: Force and Newton's laws
chapters.description: Use free-body diagrams and Newton's laws to predict acceleration and motion changes.

chapters.title: Momentum, impulse, and collisions
chapters.description: Apply conservation of momentum to collisions and recoil situations.

chapters.title: Work, energy, and power
chapters.description: Relate force to energy transfer and analyze efficiency and power in systems.

chapters.title: Uniform circular motion and gravitation
chapters.description: Model circular motion and gravitational attraction in satellites and planetary systems.

chapters.title: Wave properties and superposition
chapters.description: Describe wave quantities and apply reflection, refraction, diffraction, and interference.

chapters.title: Light, lenses, and optical models
chapters.description: Use ray and wave models to analyze image formation and optical instruments.

chapters.title: Sound, pitch, loudness, and Doppler effect
chapters.description: Connect sound wave behavior to frequency, intensity, and moving-source effects.

chapters.title: Electrostatics, electric fields, and potential
chapters.description: Model electric forces and potential differences in static-charge systems.

chapters.title: Circuits and domestic electricity
chapters.description: Analyze circuit behavior, power consumption, and safety in household systems.

chapters.title: Magnetism and electromagnetic induction
chapters.description: Explain magnetic effects of current and induction in generators and transformers.

chapters.title: Radiation, radioactivity, and half-life
chapters.description: Classify ionizing radiation and model radioactive decay quantitatively.

chapters.title: Nuclear models, fission, and fusion
chapters.description: Compare fission and fusion through binding energy and mass-energy conversion.

chapters.title: Astronomy and space science
chapters.description: Study celestial coordinates, orbital motion, and cosmology foundations.

chapters.title: Atomic world and quantum foundations
chapters.description: Bridge atomic models, photoelectric effect, and wave-particle duality.

chapters.title: Energy and use of energy
chapters.description: Evaluate energy resources, efficiency, and sustainability trade-offs.

chapters.title: Medical physics
chapters.description: Connect imaging technologies and radiation safety to physics principles.
